text:
{
  "title": "Noise-Scale Color Decisiveness",
  "group_name": "noise_scale_color_decisiveness",
  "family": "photometric_uncertainty_geometry",
  "summary": "Represent whether each object's ugriz color pattern is physically decisive or noise-ambiguous relative to canonical SDSS photometric precision, making borderline star, galaxy, and quasar color overlaps explicit without using labels.",
  "depends_on": [],
  "strategy": "Use only the five observed magnitudes. Adopt a fixed per-band noise scale of 0.03 mag from the SDSS quasar catalog description of typical five-band photometric accuracy (https://heasarc.gsfc.nasa.gov/w3browse/all/sdssquasar.html), with color noise sigma_color = sqrt(2) * 0.03; for adjacent colors u-g, g-r, r-i, i-z and all ten pairwise band differences, compute signed noise-normalized margins color / sigma_color clipped to [-30, 30]. Derive one coherent set of uncertainty-margin summaries: counts of strongly positive, strongly negative, and ambiguous color comparisons using absolute margins <= 1, between 1 and 3, and > 3; minimum, median, maximum, and mean absolute margin over adjacent and all-pair colors; robust sign-change count after setting ambiguous adjacent colors to zero; and a bounded confidence score such as tanh(abs(margin) / 3) aggregated across colors. Treat all input values as finite, use the same rules for train and test, and do not fit any statistics from labels or target distributions. SDSS asinh magnitude behavior provides additional context for interpreting faint-band magnitudes as noise-sensitive measurements (https://www.sdss4.org/dr14/algorithms/magnitudes/).",
  "expected_signal": "Raw colors encode direction and size, but not whether the differences are large enough to be trusted at survey precision; this group can help balanced accuracy by separating decisive continuum shapes from noise-scale color ties, especially where quasars and compact galaxies are photometrically similar to stars as noted in photometric quasar-selection background (https://arxiv.org/html/2605.18218).",
  "risk": "The fixed 0.03 mag scale is an approximation because row-level photometric errors are absent, so the features may be partly redundant with existing color and ordering groups or may overemphasize synthetic precision artifacts if the competition data do not follow real SDSS noise behavior."
}
